economic_notes: Nation is withdrawing from Western Cree Tribal Council and its Child Youth & Family Enhancement Agency. Nation owns 100% of Sturgeon Lake Developments Co. Ltd. and Sturgeon Lake Resources Ltd., and 20% of Niyanin Nations Limited Partnership. Significant wildfire evacuations impacted the Nation in prior year.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) inability to obtain financial records for investments (Sturgeon Lake Resources Ltd. and Niyanin Nations Limited Partnership had no financial records available), making it impossible to verify carrying amounts; (2) asset retirement obligations have not been estimated or recorded. Prior year (2023) restated for $1,852,265 accrual difference in accounts receivable and deferred revenue. Nation intends to withdraw from Western Cree Tribal Council, economic impact unknown. Total government transfers calculated as WCTC/ISC ($35,751,390) + ISC direct ($5,351,175) + Government of Alberta ($218,560) + CMHC ($2,751,131) - noting WCTC flows ISC funds through to the Nation so ISC and WCTC figures may overlap; used revenue line totals as presented. Operations and Maintenance expense of $30,486,561 is unusually large relative to budget of $3,002,833, likely driven by ISC fire recovery set funding of $18,839,297 recognized in the year. Three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method.
